_meta.description: Canonical bibliography sources for Noesis. Single source of truth; data/bibliography.bib and docs/BIBLIOGRAPHY.md are generated from this file.

sources.title: The magical number 4 in short-term memory: A reconsideration of mental storage capacity
supports: limited capacity of controlled working memory
low-bandwidth human controller abstraction
limits: does not provide a measured PFC bitrate
capacity estimates are task- and chunk-dependent
risk_if_overused: pretending Noesis measured biological memory capacity

sources.title: The magical number seven, plus or minus two: Some limits on our capacity for processing information
supports: bounded information-processing channel of the human controller
limits: historical estimate, superseded by chunk-based accounts
risk_if_overused: treating '7±2' as a literal bandwidth constant

sources.title: Working memory
sources.venue: The Psychology of Learning and Motivation
supports: componential model of working memory used as controller analogy
limits: model of biological memory, not of a software buffer
risk_if_overused: mapping software state onto biological subsystems literally

sources.title: An integrative theory of prefrontal cortex function
supports: cognitive control via goal representations
top-down biasing as control abstraction
limits: biological theory; Noesis implements no neural mechanism
risk_if_overused: claiming Noesis models PFC

sources.title: Cognitive control, hierarchy, and the rostro-caudal organization of the frontal lobes
supports: hierarchical cognitive control motivating layered pipeline
limits: anatomical hierarchy ≠ software pipeline hierarchy
risk_if_overused: equating module depth with cortical hierarchy

sources.title: How to measure metacognition
supports: metacognition and monitoring as a measurable construct
externalized metacognition framing
limits: measures human metacognitive sensitivity, not a tool's
risk_if_overused: claiming Noesis has metacognition rather than supporting the user's

sources.title: Metamemory: A theoretical framework and new findings
sources.venue: The Psychology of Learning and Motivation
supports: monitoring/control loop structure for reflection methods
limits: framework for human metamemory
risk_if_overused: asserting a literal monitoring/control loop in code

sources.title: A Cognitive Theory of Consciousness
supports: global workspace: broadcast and selective access as analogy
limits: not a consciousness proof
not a brain-measurement claim
risk_if_overused: claiming Noesis instantiates a global workspace

sources.title: Conscious processing and the global neuronal workspace hypothesis
supports: broadcast/re-entry dynamics as structural analogy
limits: GNWT is a neuroscientific hypothesis about consciousness; Noesis makes no such claim
risk_if_overused: using GNWT to imply biological workspace in software

sources.title: Experimental and theoretical approaches to conscious processing
supports: ignition/broadcast vocabulary for selective access analogy
limits: about neural consciousness, not software access control
risk_if_overused: importing 'ignition' as a literal mechanism

sources.title: The free-energy principle: a unified brain theory?
supports: precision-weighting and prediction-error language
action under uncertainty framing
limits: Noesis is human-in-the-loop, not an autonomous active-inference agent
risk_if_overused: claiming Noesis minimizes variational free energy

sources.title: Active Inference: The Free Energy Principle in Mind, Brain, and Behavior
supports: expected-free-energy vocabulary for action selection analogy
limits: formal autonomous agent theory; Noesis closes the loop through a human
risk_if_overused: presenting the IEV functional as a literal EFE computation

sources.title: Whatever next? Predictive brains, situated agents, and the future of cognitive science
supports: predictive-processing framing of error-driven refinement
limits: theory of biological cognition
risk_if_overused: claiming the pipeline is a predictive-coding hierarchy

sources.title: Cognition in the Wild
supports: cognition distributed across people and artifacts
externalized computation justification
limits: descriptive ethnography, not a performance guarantee
risk_if_overused: claiming distribution per se improves outcomes

sources.title: The extended mind
supports: tool-mediated cognition / cognitive extension framing
limits: philosophical thesis, contested; not an empirical result
risk_if_overused: treating extension as proven cognitive enhancement

sources.title: Cognitive offloading
supports: offloading improves immediate task performance
delegated computation framing
limits: offloading may introduce dependence and skill atrophy
risk_if_overused: claiming offloading is uniformly beneficial

sources.title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models
supports: multi-sample candidate generation and aggregation
limits: gains are task-dependent; more samples cost compute
risk_if_overused: assuming more samples always improve quality

sources.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
supports: critique-and-refine loop for candidate improvement
limits: self-feedback can entrench errors without external verification
risk_if_overused: treating self-critique as ground truth

sources.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
supports: verbal self-reflection between passes
limits: reflection quality bounded by the base model
risk_if_overused: claiming reflection yields reinforcement-learning guarantees

sources.title: Improving Factuality and Reasoning in Language Models through Multiagent Debate
supports: multi-node disagreement as a diversity signal
limits: more agents ≠ better reasoning by default
risk_if_overused: claiming debate guarantees correctness

sources.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Gen LLM Applications via Multi-Agent Conversation
supports: agent/tool orchestration patterns
limits: framework, not evidence of reasoning gains
risk_if_overused: citing as proof that orchestration improves reasoning

sources.title: Let's Verify Step by Step
supports: process supervision over outcome-only supervision
step-level verification motivating the IEV gate
limits: process reward models need labeled supervision; Noesis uses a computable proxy
risk_if_overused: presenting the IEV proxy as a trained process reward model

sources.title: Solving math word problems with process- and outcome-based feedback
supports: process- vs outcome-based feedback distinction
limits: domain-specific (math); not a general verifier
risk_if_overused: generalizing math-verifier results to all claims

sources.title: Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena
supports: LLM-as-judge as a usable but biased evaluator
limits: judge bias (position, verbosity, self-preference); not final truth
risk_if_overused: treating an LLM judge as ground truth

sources.title: A Survey on LLM-as-a-Judge
supports: survey of judge reliability and failure modes
limits: documents limits; does not solve them
risk_if_overused: citing survey breadth as endorsement of judging

sources.title: Integrated information theory: from consciousness to its physical substrate
supports: origin of the Φ vocabulary used only to bound the proxy
limits: IIT Φ is a theory of consciousness; the repo Φ-proxy measures no experience
risk_if_overused: claiming a Φ-proxy measures integrated information or experience

sources.title: Consciousness and the Social Brain
supports: attention-schema framing for one theory lens
limits: theory of attention modelling; lens is a deterministic proxy
risk_if_overused: claiming the lens implements an attention schema

sources.title: On the criteria to be used in decomposing systems into modules
supports: information hiding, modularity, decoupling
limits: principle, not a correctness proof
risk_if_overused: citing modularity as evidence of correctness

sources.title: Refactoring: Improving the Design of Existing Code (2nd ed.)
supports: regression-safe change, rollback discipline, artifact stability
limits: engineering practice, not formal verification
risk_if_overused: claiming refactoring guarantees behavior preservation

sources.title: JSON Schema Specification (Draft 2020-12)
supports: schema contracts and structural validation
limits: validates structure, not semantic truth of content
risk_if_overused: treating schema-valid as semantically correct

sources.title: Fixing Language: An Essay on Conceptual Engineering
supports: conceptual engineering: assessing and improving concepts
category/reality-map layer
limits: philosophy of concepts; no empirical metric
risk_if_overused: claiming category refactoring discovers metaphysical truth

sources.title: What is conceptual engineering and what should it be?
supports: framework for conceptual refactoring of terms
limits: meta-philosophical; bounds, not validates, the category layer
risk_if_overused: over-reading category outputs as conceptual truth

sources.title: Possible selves
supports: possible/future selves as a legitimate psychological construct
framing for an extrapolated identity trajectory
limits: describes self-representations; does not license predicting a person's future
risk_if_overused: treating a projected self as a prediction of destiny

sources.title: The psychology of life stories
supports: narrative identity: the self as an evolving internalized story
grounds EIIC as a narrative-trajectory construct
limits: a descriptive theory of identity; no predictive or diagnostic power
risk_if_overused: presenting a narrative construct as a measured or predicted identity

sources.title: Process and Reality
supports: process-over-substance framing as bounded analogy only
limits: speculative metaphysics; used strictly as analogy, never as mechanism
risk_if_overused: civilizational essentialism or mystical reading
